description: This 3D chart illustrates the calculated relationship between yield strength improvement (Δσₚ, unit: MPa, z-axis), carbide size (unit: nm, x-axis), and carbide volume fraction f (unit: %, y-axis) in Fe–Mn–C–Al low-density steel. Experimentally reported data points for different carbide types, including TiC, V(C,N), V₄C₃, VC/Fe₃C, VC, and NbC, are plotted on the calculated surface, with corresponding literature citation numbers marked in brackets. The trend shows that higher volume fraction and smaller size of carbide precipitates lead to more significant yield strength improvement, with the maximum Δσₚ approaching 900 MPa. This figure supports the core conclusion that carbide precipitates act as effective obstacles to dislocation slip during plastic deformation, delivering notable precipitation strengthening effect to enhance the mechanical performance of Fe–Mn–C–Al steel.
